error_patterns: Dominant error: m_DELAI modifier applied to S02_INJONCTION records without any numeric deadline in constats_body (no 'sous X jours/mois' or explicit date). Hunter over-applied m_DELAI to essentially every formal demand, including vague formulations like 'a l'occasion de la prochaine revision', 'dans les meilleurs delais', 'des reception', or open-ended requests to 'justifier'/'transmettre' without any stated timeframe. Checked all 39 S02_INJONCTION+m_DELAI records lacking a delay signal in Hunter's own reasoning text against the corpus; confirmed 23 true false positives (delay absent) and 16 true positives where the reasoning simply omitted a delay that was present in the body (e.g. 'delai d'un mois', 'sous 3 mois', 'avant fin 2024', a specific calendar date). No instances of the R10_TENSION/unfulfilled-promise pattern were found — R10_TENSION is not used anywhere in this batch (0 occurrences), so that systematic error type does not apply here. Spot-check of 20 high-confidence records and full re-read of both medium-confidence records found no other errors: no false M08, no false M14, no gravity or trajectory mistakes (T1_PREMIER records were grepped for prior-inspection language with no hits), no missed multi-label, no over-aggressive M17, no wrong R code, and no R02 over-attribution (all 4 R02_PROMESSE records genuinely describe unverified future commitments, not factual reports).
verdict: Good overall quality (structurally 100% valid — no invalid codes, no joined single-value fields, no modifiers misplaced in mechanisms, all required fields present on all 130 records). The one systematic issue is m_DELAI over-application on ~35% of S02_INJONCTION records (23/62), consistent with the known batch pattern; corrected in final-58.json. No M15_AUTRE usage. All other axes (domain, dynamic, actor, stage, gravity, trajectory) held up under spot-check and targeted re-reads.
